subject: Mozi Minute: The Three Stonecutters
body:
Words I like: Culture is the spoken and unspoken rules that govern reward and punishment within an organization.

Mozi Minute: The Three Stone Cutters - Why Vision Makes You More

Three stonecutters were working side by side. A passerby asks them what they're doing.

First one's hunched over, replies:

"I'm cutting stone. Hate every second."

Second one's methodical, replies:

"Paying the bills. It's honest work."

Third one's different. Eyes bright, staying late:

"I'm building a cathedral that will stand for centuries. My grandchildren will see these spires pierce the heavens."

Same job. Same pay.

Wildly different results.

Companies obsess over "productivity hacks" and "performance management" but miss the biggest lever in business: vision.

That's what unlocks discretionary effort.

I define discretionary effort as work above the amount required to maintain employment.

This is the biggest unlockable resource in an organization.

It's the difference between being on a mission and clocking in / out.

You unlock discretionary effort with a clear vision and a culture that rewards excellence. Aka - talk about the cathedral, not the act of cutting stones.

"The difference between a job and a calling isn't the work - it's the worker's perspective." - Peter Drucker

This is why great cultures outperform great strategies.

As promised, value in under a minute.

Build cathedrals, not cubicles,

Alex
